name: Gloria Lynne
abstract: Gloria Lynne (born Gloria Wilson; November 23, 1929 – October 15, 2013), also known as Gloria Alleyne, was an American jazz vocalist with a recording career spanning from 1958 to 2007.

concerts.label: Ninth Annual WLIB Festival of Negro Music and Drama
Dick Gregory with Gloria Lynne and Mongo Santamaria

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Ernestine Anderson, Etta James, Gloria Lynne, I mean we played “Watermelon Man” in A flat.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Eldridge
about_quotes.evidence: And after Dolores left, there was another girl who joined. I think her old man used to play the saxophone or something. Gloria...Gloria...Gloria something. It wasn't Gloria Lynne; that's a Black singer.

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: I've got Gloria Lynn going into Vegas at the Thunderbird for eight weeks. I want you to have the band. And I'd get the band together, go there with Gloria Lynn.

about_quotes.speaker: Melba Liston
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWEE: Gloria Lynne’s thing. ... INTERVIEWEE: The difference is who picks what songs. Dinah picks some soul songs. Gloria picks some . . .
about_quotes.doc_title: Melba Liston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Red Holloway
about_quotes.evidence: that’s another person that’s alive now, that’s still alive, Gloria Lynne, she’s another that has that certain — you don’t hear much about her.
about_quotes.doc_title: Red Holloway interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: He says, 'With everybody that I'm getting on this show you've written for.' Joe Williams, Gloria Lynn, Duke Ellington.
